Title: Coalescence and directed anisotropic growth of starch granule initials in subdomains of Arabidopsis thaliana chloroplasts

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Starch granule initiation and granule growth patterning within chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated α-1,4-glucan chains contributing to starch granule initials
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to α-1,4-glucan chains during starch synthesis; additionally controls starch granule initiation and anisotropic granule growth via its non-enzymatic N-terminus
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for efficient initiation of multiple starch granule initials and for correct anisotropic growth shaping starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for granule initiation and morphology
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major plant glucose storage polymer and major dietary carbohydrate/calorie source
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch granule initiation and is required for anisotropic lenticular granule growth; loss causes few large rounded granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis ss4 mutant analysis; transgenic complementation with bacterial glycogen synthase and SS4 N-terminus fusion; 13CO2 pulse-chase labeling; SBF-SEM; NanoSIMS imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in leaf mesophyll chloroplasts during daytime transitory starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stromal pockets associated with thylakoid subdomains
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Starch synthase family glycosyltransferase; C-terminal glucosyltransferase domain plus non-enzymatic N-terminal domain
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark regime; extended night de-starching; daytime photosynthesis; 13CO2 labeling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of SS4 causes severe reduction in granule number and abnormal rounded morphology
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to engineer starch granule number and morphology; N-terminal localization domain may be exploited to direct glucan-synthesizing activity for tailored starch structure
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper shows the non-enzymatic N-terminus is sufficient, when fused to bacterial glycogen synthase, to restore anisotropic growth and lenticular morphology by directing subchloroplastic localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS4 is essential for efficient starch granule initiation in Arabidopsis chloroplasts. 2. SS4 is critically required for anisotropic equatorial growth that generates lenticular granules. 3. The N-terminal non-enzymatic region controls localization and can confer proper growth patterning when fused to a heterologous glycogen synthase.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch-specific polymer synthesis step within granule matrix
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes essentially linear α-1,4-glucan chains (amylose) from ADP-glucose within the starch granule matrix
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a starch component, amylose, deposited in granule cores
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Minor starch component influencing starch structure and properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for amylose deposition inside starch granule cores; gbss mutant lacks amylose and shows reduced internal 13C enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gbss mutant analysis; 13CO2 labeling at midday; NanoSIMS comparison of granule core versus surface enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in leaf chloroplast starch granules during daytime starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 h light/12 h dark regime; midday 13CO2 pulse labeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for modifying amylose content and starch physicochemical properties in crops
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study provides in vivo evidence that GBSS-mediated amylose synthesis occurs inside the granule rather than mainly at the surface
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBSS synthesizes amylose within the starch granule core in vivo. 2. gbss mutant granules retain surface labeling but show reduced core enrichment, demonstrating loss of internal amylose deposition. 3. GBSS acts as the defining enzyme for the amylose component of starch.
